_comment: Matches from audit-skill-content.js that a human read in full and judged benign. Anchored to the sha256 of the matched line: the exception survives the line moving, but dies the moment its content changes — so nobody can edit malicious text in under an approved exception. Reviewed 2026-07-14.

reviewed.reason: Our own anti-injection rule, quoting the attacker's phrasing as an example of what to REFUSE. The line teaches the agent to report such text, not to obey it.
Same rule, same purpose: lists "do not tell the user" among the phrases that are a FINDING TO REPORT. Quoting an attack is not performing it.

Read in full: 'Do not show the user the helper's JSON output; only the human-readable trend line and the written path.' This suppresses raw JSON noise in favour of a readable summary — the result and the file path ARE shown. It hides formatting, not actions.

Read in full: a diagnostic message printed TO THE USER explaining why a no-TTY subprocess cannot reach their own `claude /login` credentials in the macOS Keychain, and how to fix it (`claude setup-token`). It neither reads nor transmits any credential; it explains an auth failure.
